title: Implement BLE transport for Python TUI native client
description: Why this exists: native apps need a direct BLE path for laptop and mobile-like usage where IP over BLE is impractical. What needs to be done: implement BLE scanning, device filtering, connection, service discovery, MTU-aware writes, notification subscription, reconnect handling, timeout handling, and pairing/user-selection behavior using a cross-platform Python BLE library such as Bleak where feasible. Acceptance criteria: works on Linux BlueZ and macOS CoreBluetooth for standard GATT client operations; service/characteristic UUIDs are configurable or derived from the native interface spec; disconnect/reconnect is handled without wedging the UI; tests use mocks/fakes where hardware is absent; hardware smoke-test Bead is created if real-device validation is missing.
